title: Array depth defined with the num method of an enum where the variable name is identical to part of the enum instance name causes an error
body:
The title is a bit of a mouthful, hopefully an example will make it clear:

``` verilog
module test_mod ();
 
    typedef enum logic [4:0] {ENUM_ELEM1, ENUM_ELEM2} test_enum_t;

    test_enum_t test_mem_addr_e;
    logic test_mem [test_mem_addr_e.num()];
    logic test_mem_int [test_mem_addr_e.num()];

endmodule 
```

Running `iverilog` on the above throws the following error:

```
test2.sv:8: error: No function named `test_mem_addr_e.num' found in this context (test_mod).
test2.sv:8: error: Dimensions must be constant.
test2.sv:8       : This size expression violates the rule: test_mem_addr_e.num()
2 error(s) during elaboration.
```

If you append any text to `test_mem`, as long as does not match `test_mem_addr_e`, no error is generated. 

I tested this using 2948e9a89f706144eab61dffc5642c959a019b8e.
